description: Work queue for proof DAG extraction in the Maynard-Tao bounded-gaps DAG.

queue.reason: Polymath8b — improves Maynard 600 → 246 via ε-enlarged simplex.
Polymath8a — MPZ window 600ϖ+180δ<7 driving Polymath8b's unconditional bounds.
GPY 'Primes in Tuples I' — direct ancestor; 1D Selberg sieve Maynard generalizes.
Maynard's large gaps — same multidimensional sieve, opposite problem.
FGKT 'Long gaps between primes' — independent simultaneous large-gaps proof.
Maynard 'Dense clusters of primes in subsets' — abstract framework version of 1311.4600.
Ford-Maynard-Tao 'Chains of large gaps' — synthesis of FGKT and Maynard's large-gaps papers.
Goldston-Pintz-Yildirim 'Primes in Tuples II' — quantitative refinement of GPY I.
Banks-Freiberg-Maynard limit points — quantitative density of normalized prime gap limits.
Pintz on Polignac numbers — application of bounded gaps to Polignac's conjecture.
Castillo-Hall-Lemke Oliver-Pollack-Thompson — Maynard's framework over number fields and function fields.
Maynard 'Primes with restricted digits' — same author's sieve methods on a digit-restricted problem; demonstrates framework generality.
Maynard PIAP I — extends BV beyond 1/2 without the smooth-modulus restriction of Polymath8a. Almost-all level 1/2 + 1/55.
Maynard PIAP II — pushes BFI's well-factorable level from 4/7 to 3/5 via triply well-factorable hypothesis.
Maynard PIAP III: Uniform residue classes — completes the trilogy.
Possible Zhang preprint id — Zhang submitted directly to Annals of Math; arxiv preprint may not exist.
queue.blocked_reason: Need to verify existence of an arxiv version.
